1999-03-01.title: Ethiopia Wins Border War Against Eritrea
1999-03-01.abstract: Ethiopia declares total victory over Eritrea in border conflict after four-day offensive dislodged Eritrean army from remote dusty borderland in Bademe region; Eritrea says it has no plans for counteroffensive (M)

1999-03-01.title: Drug Tourism / In Laos, English Menus and New Opium Dens : Westerners Flock East for an 'Asian Trip'
1999-03-01.abstract: VANG VIENG, Laos—  With a ring of opium grease starting to bubble around the pinhole on the end of his pipe, the emaciated old man reclining on a pile of dirty white pillows nods slightly to signal the liquid will soon vaporize.

1999-03-01.title: U.S. Says It Caught a Chinese Smuggler Seeking Gyroscopes That Can Guide Missiles
1999-03-01.abstract: Federal agents in California arrest Yao Yi, Chinese citizen, and charge him with trying to obtain component vital to missile guidance systems; arrest sheds light on China's alleged effort to smuggle American technology to improve accuracy of its weapons (M)

1999-03-01.title: Death Taboo Weakening, Japan Sees 1st Transplant
1999-03-01.abstract: Doctors in Japan, for first time, perform legal heart transplant striking at taboo on taking organs from patients whom Japanese traditionally do not consider dead; operation on man in 40's is ethical and emotional milestone in country that has long believed that death comes only after heart stops beating and body turns cold (M)

1999-03-01.title: Amid Political Interference, The Euro Has Yet to Foster A Unified Monetary Policy : Initial Luster Of New Money Begins to Fade For Europe
1999-03-01.abstract: Two months after its rollout into the market, the euro is well into the process of becoming a no-longer-new-currency. The long haul is setting in.

1999-03-02.title: 9 Die at Indonesian Mosque When Police Fire at Rioters
1999-03-02.abstract: Nine people die in Ambon, Indonesia, after police open fire on crowd outside mosque; shootings follow new round of clashes between Muslims and Christians in provincial capital, on island 1,400 miles east of Jakarta; photo (M)

1999-03-02.title: U.N. Panel Urges Tribunal For Khmer Rouge Leaders
1999-03-02.abstract: UN panel recommends that new international tribunal be convened to try about dozen senior Khmer Rouge officials on charges of crimes against humanity while they ruled Cambodia; under recommendation, separate Cambodian truth commission would consider all other cases; China informs Clinton Administration it will veto idea if brought before UN Security Council; Khmer Rouge relied almost exclusively on Chinese support during their rule, which led to deaths of nearly two million Cambodians between 1975 and 1979 (M)

1999-03-02.title: Tourists Kidnapped
1999-03-02.abstract: 13 foreign tourists and unknown number of Ugandans are kidnapped while tracking mountain gorillas in forest near Uganda's southwestern border; three are Americans; attackers appear to be Rwandans (S)

1999-03-02.title: Man in the News; Nigerian Question Mark: Olusegun Obasanjo
1999-03-02.abstract: Man-in-the-News profile of Gen Olusegun Obasanjo, who will become Nigeria's first elected President after 16 years of military dictatorships; he is anomaly in country's recent history, and man of contradictions; he is best known as only one to have relinquished power voluntarily, out of eight generals to rule Nigeria since independence from Britain in 1960; caused resentment for governing with strong hand before 1979, but gained respect after being jailed in 1995 as outspoken critic of military dictatorship; was released from prison eight months ago; photo (M)

1999-03-02.title: Israelis on Border Are Anxious, but Area Is Quiet
1999-03-02.abstract: Anxious quiet returns to northernmost area of Israel following Israeli bombardment of guerrilla targets across border in Lebanon; residents of Kibbutz Maayan Baruch are allowed to emerge from underground shelters for first time since week of Hezbollah attacks left seven Israeli's dead and led to sharpest Isralei offensive deep inside Lebanon since 1996; photo; map (M)

1999-03-03.title: 8 Tourists Slain in Uganda, Including U.S. Couple
1999-03-03.abstract: Eight foreign tourists, including American couple, are slain in Bwindi Impenetrable Forest of Uganda by band of Hutu rebels, who are said to be remnants of ethnic Hutu militias that carried out mass killings in Rwanda in 1994; four Ugandan park employees are also slain; victims include four Britons and two New Zealanders; Robert Haubner and his wife Susan Miller, slain American couple, were executives for Intel Corp; attack on group of 31 tourists appears to be related to violence that has beset central Africa for years; survivors say as many as 150 Hutu soldiers sought out Americans and Britons among tourists; map; photos (M)

1999-03-03.title: Hopes for Kosovo Deal Rise As a Guerrilla Chief Resigns
1999-03-03.abstract: Kosovo Liberation Army political leader Adem Demaci resigns, raising hopes among diplomats that ethnic Albanian guerrilla movement will sign autonomy plan proposed at peace talks; Demaci opposes peace plan, which would grant Kosovo autonomy under protection of NATO-led military force; Yugoslav tank and mortar fire pounds southern Kosovo hillsides, striking at rebel positions and driving thousands of ethnic Albanian civilians from their homes in some of heaviest fighting since start of talks; US and NATO officials say Yugoslav military buildup in Kosovo is violation of cease-fire reached in Oct, but that NATO will probably refrain from punitive strikes to avoid undermining efforts to reach accord; map (M)

1999-03-03.title: Ethiopia Is Celebrating, But Perhaps Too Soon
1999-03-03.abstract: Ethiopian troops recently expelled Eritrean Army from Badme region, which they have been fighting over for nearly year; Eritrea says it is willing to accept cease-fire, but Ethiopia has not said whether it will end hostilities (M)

1999-03-03.title: NATO Said to Rule Out Kosovo Reaction
1999-03-03.abstract: Clinton Administration officials and NATO diplomats say that US and its allies have no intention of launching punitive strikes against Yugoslav military targets at this point despite army buildup and renewed fighting in Kosovo (M)

1999-03-03.title: Croatia Branded as Another Balkans Pariah
1999-03-03.abstract: Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe report condemns Croatian Government of Pres Franjo Tudjman for violating human rights and rights of minorities and disregarding rule of law (M)

1999-03-04.title: Ruling Party, at 70, Tries Hard to Cling To Power in Mexico
1999-03-04.abstract: Analysts for years have been predicting demise of Mexico's Institutional Revolutionary Party (PRI), but its recent election victories have undermined those arguments; debate over party's past and future dominate Mexico as Pres Ernesto Zedillo and 2,000 party stalwarts prepare to celebrate its 70th anniversary on March 4; party for decades was tool of successive presidents using authoritarian methods to insure one-party rule; over last 20 years, opposition parties have chipped away at monopoly and currently control Congress and 10 of 32 state capitals; dozens of PRI leaders have defected to opposition; PRI has never lost presidency; polls suggest that party can win presidential election next year, but only if it can avoid damaging split; Zedillo says that unlike his predecessors he will not handpick his successor; photo (M)

1999-03-04.title: Opoku Ware II, King of Asante, Is Dead at 89
1999-03-04.abstract: Otumfuo Opoku Ware II, traditional King of the Asante people of Ghana, dies at age 89; photo; map (M)

1999-03-04.title: Terror Suspect Said to Anger Afghan Hosts
1999-03-04.abstract: Senior US officials say suspected terrorist Osama bin Laden and Taliban, his protectors in Afghanistan, have had violent falling-out, raising possibility that his days of refuge may be numbered; say fight broke out between bin Laden's bodyguards and Taliban officers assigned to watch over him; say bin Laden was expelled from Kandahar; say he was isolated in countryside and stripped of his satellite telephones, which allow him to plot with fellow radicals throughout world; Taliban has shown no sign that it is willing to deliver bin Laden to US; Taliban official says Afghanistan has sent emissary to US asking how to deal with him (M)

1999-03-04.title: MIFFED AT EUROPE, U.S. RAISES TARIFFS FOR LUXURY GOODS
1999-03-04.abstract: US heats up two politically contentious trade disputes with Europe, slapping 100 percent tariffs on $520 million in European products and threatening to ban Concorde from landing in US; tariffs imposed by Clinton Administration on luxury goods are part of six-year battle over trade in bananas; threat to ban Concorde is in bill passed overwhelmingly in House, in retaliation for European ruling that would ban many older American airplanes from landing in Europe; both battles are really about tensions that have erupted between US and Europe newly unified by common currency; Washington and European Union find themselves unable to resolve seemingly ordinary economic disputes, with each accusing other of defying World Trade Organization (M)

1999-03-04.title: World Briefing
1999-03-04.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    ISRAEL: NETANYAHU ON PULLOUT  Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he would consider ''the possibility of a unilateral withdrawal'' from Lebanon if ''accompanied by necessary security measures.'' He rejected a suggestion by Foreign Minister Ariel Sharon that May elections be postponed in order to form a unity government dedicated to a pullout. Both men's remarks followed a campaign promise by the Labor Party candidate, Ehud Barak, to withdraw troops within a year if elected Prime Minister.   Deborah Sontag (NYT)

1999-03-05.title: China Grimly Pledges to Push On With Reforms
1999-03-05.abstract: Prime Min Zhu Rongji says China will push on with economic and social reforms despite 'grim economic picture'; says China will continue to open country wider to outside world; his sobering speech before Parliament enumerates China's many current problems--from unemployment to official corruption to flagging export market; says China will continue recent policy of massive deficit spending to bolster economy and insulate itself from worst of Asia's economic woes; predicts China will have economic growth of 'around 7 percent' in 1999, down from official 7.8 percent in 1998 (M)

1999-03-05.title: Albright Takes Some Heat For Rambouillet 'Success'
1999-03-05.abstract: PARIS—  Secretary of State Madeleine Albright's personal efforts staved off failure at least temporarily in the Kosovo peace talks last month at Rambouillet, France, but her intensive, often improvised, diplomacy raised concerns that her entry into the talks clouded the prospects for settling the civil war between Serbs and ethnic Albanians when the two sides reconvene on March 15, according to officials in Europe and the United States.

1999-03-05.title: No End to Violence in Ulster: Each Side Now Kills Its Own
1999-03-05.abstract: Protestants and Catholics opposed to promise of peace in Northern Ireland are killing and maiming their own; members of paramilitary armies who control working-class neighborhoods are turning their violence on their own, in vicious effort to avoid being swept away in march toward peace; in grim paradox, beatings increase at times like present, when cease-fires and political negotiations drastically reduce killing between two warring religious communities and bring exhilarating promise of peace; Sam Cushnahan, director of Families Against Intimidation and Terror, nonsectarian organization, says new violence is from militias that took on enforcement role to maintain unity and discipline in their communities as sectarian conflict became more intense; says they do not want to give up this power and control; photos (M)

1999-03-05.title: World Briefing
1999-03-05.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    JORDAN: NEW CABINET SWORN IN -- King Abdullah swore in a new 23-member Cabinet, ridding the Government of officials who were loyal to Prince Hassan, the uncle once expected to become King. Abdullah, 37, installed Abdul-Raouf Rawabdeh, left, as Prime Minister, and urged him and the rest of the Cabinet to consolidate ties with Israel and to work for a more open government and a freer press.   (AP)    IRAN: DRIVE ON DISSIDENT -- A special religious court barred Iran's newspapers from even mentioning Ayatollah Hossein-Ali Montazeri, a prominent liberal dissident who regularly denounces the conservatives' hold on Iran. Mr. Montazeri, who has been under police surveillance for years, was once favored to become Iran's supreme leader.   (Agence France-Presse)

1999-03-05.title: Ukraine's Ex-Premier Says He's Closer to Gaining U.S. Asylum
1999-03-05.abstract: Pavlo Lazarenko, former Ukrainian Prime Minister who asserts that his nation's leaders are using corruption charges to persecute him, says he has cleared first procedural hurdle toward winning political asylum in United States; Ukraine's prosecutor general tells news service that he is confident of Lazarenko's eventual return to Ukraine for prosecution; says Ukraine has given US evidence that Lazarenko embezzled and misused millions of dollars in government funds as part of long-running corruption scheme; he has been in Federal custody in New York since Feb 19, when he arrived at Kennedy International Airport with invalid visa; photo (M)

1999-03-06.title: Issue of Troops in Lebanon Energizes Israeli Election
1999-03-06.abstract: Labor party candidate Ehud Barak injects actual issue, withdrawal of forces from Lebanon, into Israel's volatile, personality-based election campaign; polls show voters are suspicious that such promises capitalize on grief at recent deaths of seven Israelis; Barak promises to withdraw army in one year, Yitzhak Mordechai says he would act even sooner, and Ariel Sharon calls for delaying election altogether; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu opposes such deadlines, preferring to seek Lebanese cooperation rather than dealing with Syria (S)

1999-03-06.title: China Reaffirms Drive to Overhaul Bloated State Industries
1999-03-06.abstract: Senior Chinese officials reaffirm determination to squeeze dead wood and red ink out of bloated state-owned enterprises, vowing to make nearly all competitive and profitable by end of 1999; Zhu Rongji, reporting on first year as prime minister, says he will press ahead with overhaul and streamlining of bureaucracy despite 'grim environment at home and abroad'; implicitly acknowledges social unrest that has flared as urban unemployment grows and farmers protest taxes and corruption; projects seven percent economic growth for 1999 (S)

1999-03-06.title: Lord Denning, 100, a Populist Who Enlivened British Courts
1999-03-06.abstract: Lord Denning, prominent British judge who often favored precepts of common sense over starchier ones of common law, dies at age 100; his handling of inquiry into 1963 Profumo scandal recalled, as well as decisions protecting individuals from bureaucrats, corporations and trade unions; photo (M)

1999-03-06.title: Italian Presses Clinton on Pilot's Acquittal
1999-03-06.abstract: Italian Prime Min Massimo D'Alema, in Washington, tells Pres Clinton he is shocked by acquittal of American pilot in deaths of 20 tourists who died when ski lift cable was cut by US military plane; says, at joint news conference, that Italians and Europeans demand accountability and punishment; Clinton apologizes, says US is not trying to duck responsibility, and reviews procedural changes to prevent such incidents; visit coincides with verdict in court martial of Richard Ashby, who says Marine Corps refused to deliver crew's letter of sympathy to victims' families (S)

1999-03-06.title: Albright, on Visit to Indonesia, Meets Detained Timor Leader
1999-03-06.abstract: Sec Madeleine Albright meets with East Timorese separatist leader Xanana Gusmao, who is brought from house arrest to Indonesian Foreign Ministryfor occasion; Albright hears talks with Gusmao and Pres B J Habibie on desperately poor region's transition to independence (S)

1999-03-07.title: Unforeseen, Strife Eases For Algeria
1999-03-07.abstract: Algerians have new hope of peace following savage war between country's military rulers and Islamic guerrilla groups that took at least 80,000 lives since 1992; presidential election is scheduled for Apr 15, with 11 candidates representing array of political opinions, and promise from army not to interfere, as it has in past; many Algerians conclude that faction among generals are so shocked by years of violence that they decided to begin transferring some power to elected politicians; senior Government officials say there can be no final answer until country rebuilds its economy and finds work for millions of unemployed, especially in 70 percent of population under 30, commonly described as 'standing reserve force' for guerrillas; photos; map (L)

1999-03-07.title: Sheik Isa, 65, Emir of Bahrain Who Built Non-Oil Economy
1999-03-07.abstract: Emir of Bahrain, Sheik Isa bin Salman al-Khalifa, dies of heart attack at age 65 following meeting with visiting Defense Sec William S Cohen (M)

1999-03-07.title: New Era for Region As Clinton Sets Visit
1999-03-07.abstract: When President Clinton visits El Salvador, Nicaragua, Honduras and Guatemala over four days this week, he will find a region forged by one tragedy or another over the last several decades, and more often than not forgotten in its intervals of renewal. The trip begins tomorrow, the day after Salvadorans vote on a new President, further cementing a remarkable democratic transformation after 12 years of civil war. Compared with the clamor of the 1980's, when the region's upheavals dominated American foreign policy, that change has occurred nearly silently.  Article, page 8.

1999-03-07.title: Clinton to Begin Visit to Central America
1999-03-07.abstract: Pres Clinton will begin four-day visit to Central America, with stops in El Salvador, Nicaragua, Honduras and Guatemala, beginning Mar 8; region's upheaval dominated American foreign policy in 1980's; now, it has taken natural disaster--Hurricane Mitch--to refocus attention there; Clinton's agenda for this visit will reflect global issues of today--free trade, immigration and drug trafficking, in addition to repairing damage from Hurricane Mitch; but past interventions and traumas are likely to confront Clinton in places like Guatemala, where truth commission recently blamed country's military rulers, assisted by Washington, for acts of genocide against country's Maya population during 36-year war; photo; map (M)

1999-03-07.title: Odd Twosome Takes On South Africa's Status Quo
1999-03-07.abstract: South Africa's first multiracial party, United Democratic Movement party, was formed by Bantu Holomisa, black man, and Roelf Meyer, who is white; recent poll suggests they may already have won over as much as 9 percent of voters; that is twice goal they set when they formed party year ago; they operate on shoestring, mostly relying on volunteers, as they campaign around country; nobody expects Holomisa-Meyer team to win South Africa's second post-apartheid elections, scheduled for June 2; but they may push aside crumbling former National Party and Zulu-focused Inkatha Freedom Party to become biggest opposition party; some political analysts even wonder if they might some day challenge African National Congress party of Pres Nelson Mandela; photo (M)

1999-03-08.title: U.S. Offers Saudi Arabia New Weapons
1999-03-08.abstract: United States Defense Sec William S Cohen, touring region to solidify anti-Iraqi sentiment among friendly Arab government, offers to sell Saudi Arabia sophisticated air-to-air missiles to help shore up its defenses against Iraq, meeting with Prince Sultan bin Abd al-Aziz; Iraq charges US pressures and blackmails Arabs (M)

1999-03-08.title: Russia Promises to Punish Chechnya for Abduction of General
1999-03-08.abstract: Interior Min Sergei Stepashin warns of economic, political and 'operational' moves to punish separatist Chechnya region unless Chechen kidnappers quickly free Maj Gen Gennadi Shpigun; kidnappers seized him from plane on ground in Grozny; Russian officials are evacuated by convoy from region (M)

1999-03-08.title: Free Trade in Fresh Water? Canada Says No and Halts Exports
1999-03-08.abstract: Canada, which contains 20 percent of world's known freshwater resources, has set temporary nationwide ban on bulk exports of fresh water; says moratorium will remain in effect until all 10 provinces can come up with way to ban exports permanently; Min Lloyd Axworthy cites concern that water could become commercial good covered by North American Free Trade Agreement, and therefore subject to continental exploitation (M)

1999-03-08.title: Ronald Willetts, 83, of Britain, Expert on Life in Ancient Crete
1999-03-08.abstract: Prof Ronald Willetts, British authority on life in ancient Crete, dies at age 83 (S)

1999-03-08.title: Are Airline Alliances Passenger-Unfriendly?
1999-03-08.abstract: IMAGINE arriving at the airport to find that instead of the familiar airline check-in desks, there is just one long row of desks displaying the logo Star Alliance. At other airports, you might have a choice of Oneworld or Atlantic Excellence. But this is Hamburg, a Star Alliance hub.

1999-03-09.title: U.S. Fires Scientist Suspected Of Giving China Bomb Data
1999-03-09.abstract: Energy Sec Bill Richardson says Wen Ho Lee, Taiwan-born scientist at Los Alamos National Laboratory, was fired for security breaches after FBI questioned him in connection with China's suspected theft of US nuclear secrets; accuses Lee of 'stonewalling' investigation; Lee has not been charged with crime, but he has been identified by Federal officials as prime suspect in spying; China denies any such theft; Sen Fred Thompson criticizes Clinton Administration for failing to notify Congress in 1997 that China had stolen nuclear secrets when Administration certified that Beijing was no longer helping other nations build nuclear bombs (M)

1999-03-09.title: Istanbul Journal; Film Confronts a History Few Turks Can Agree On
1999-03-09.abstract: Turkish filmmaker Reis Celik has produced film about three college dropouts who tried to set off Marxist revolution in Turkey, but who were captured and hanged in 1972; film, Goodbye Tomorrow, focuses on most prominent of three, Deniz Gezmis; since film's release, debates over Gezmis, leftist movement and death penalty have come to surface in Istanbul; photos (M)

1999-03-09.title: U.S. Can't Solve Every Crisis, He Says, Urging Quick Action : Blair Presses Case For Europe Force
1999-03-09.abstract: LONDON—  Correction published in the International Herald Tribune, March 11, 1999:

1999-03-09.title: Man in the News: Francisco Guillermo Flores Perez; New Salvadoran Puzzle
1999-03-09.abstract: Biographical sketch of El Salvador's newly elected Pres Francisco Flores notes that he was born in Santa Ana on Oct 17, 1959, and holds bachelor's degree in political science from Amherst College; photo (M)

1999-03-09.title: William McGonagle, 73, Hero Of Israel Attack on the Liberty
1999-03-09.abstract: William L McGonagle, who as Navy captain won Medal of Honor for maintaining control of technical research ship Liberty although wounded when ship was attacked by Israel in 1967 Arab-Israeli war, dies at age 73; photo (M)

1999-03-10.title: N.E.A. Couldn't Tell a Book by Its Cover
1999-03-10.abstract: National Endowment for Arts Chmn William J Ivey cancels grant for The Story of Color after learning from reporter that bilingual children's book was written by Mexican Zapatista guerrilla Subcomandante Marcos and bears picture of him wearing ammunition belt and mask; overrules multilayered, yearlong grant approval process; expresses concern that NEA funds might aid rebels; decision stuns Texas publisher, Cinco Puntos Press, shoestring operation that laid out $15,000 to print books; Bobby Byrd, who runs company, says he made it clear in grant proposal that guerrillas would not get NEA money; photos (M)

1999-03-10.title: China Export Charges
1999-03-10.abstract: Federal grand jury indicts Yao Yi, Chinese scientist, and Collin Shu on charges of conspiring to violate export control laws by attempting to ship fiberoptic gyroscopes to China; Customs Service holds shipments could have helped China improve accuracy of its missiles (S)

1999-03-10.title: Laguna San Ignacio Journal; Save the Whales? Mine the Salt? Mexican Standoff
1999-03-10.abstract: Proposal by Mitsubishi Corp to build huge salt plant in partnership with Mexican Government in Laguna San Ignacio pits it against environmentalists seeking to protect gray whale's winter breeding grounds; both sides have launched sophisticated, global campaigns, with videos, Web sites, letter-writing drives and guided tours to promote its view; top scientists, recruited by both sides, are divided on issues; lagoon is part of biosphere reserve created by Mexico in 1988 as largest protected natural area in Latin America; map; photo (M)

1999-03-10.title: Political Battle: What to Reveal On China Arms
1999-03-10.abstract: Clinton Administration and Congressional committee are wrangling over how much to make public of committee report on China's acquisition of sensitive American military technology; bipartisan committee found China harmed national security over last 20 years by stealing nuclear secrets and acquiring other American technology; dispute is taking on more partisan tone, providing ammunition for critics of Pres Clinton's China policy in days before key United States-China talks; committee members comment (M)

1999-03-10.title: World Briefing
1999-03-10.abstract: THE AMERICAS    ARGENTINA: A ROYAL RECONCILIATION  Prince Charles, who is making a three-day state visit, paid homage to Argentine troops who died in the 1982 Falkland Island war and shook hands with survivors, who asked him to help them visit Argentine war graves on the disputed islands. The Prince, seen left with President Carlos Saul Menem, laid a wreath at the monument to Argentina's 652 war dead, echoing a similar gesture of reconciliation by Mr. Menem in London last year.   (Reuters)    BOLIVIA: JUDGES SUSPENDED  The Government announced that it is suspending 18 judges and magistrates in several cities on suspicion that they are connected to drug dealers or have given traffickers special treatment in their courts.   Clifford Krauss (NYT)

1999-03-11.title: A New Breed in Paris:Hyper-Realistic Designers
1999-03-11.abstract: PARIS—  If there is a common link to the young designers working in Paris right now — Jeremy Scott, Bless, Gaspard Yurkievich, Sharon Wauchob — it is that they all belong to a school of hyper-realism.

1999-03-11.title: After 2d Day With Milosevic, Envoy Reports No Progress
1999-03-11.abstract: US envoy Richard C Holbrooke, after meeting with Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic, says he has failed to win any change in Serbian leader's opposition to peace proposal for Kosovo (M)

1999-03-11.title: House to Vote on Kosovo Force Despite White House Appeals
1999-03-11.abstract: House Speaker J Dennis Hastert, despite Clinton Administration's objections, says House will vote on whether to endorse sending US peacekeeping troops to Kosovo if settlement is reached there (M)

1999-03-11.title: U.S. Curbs Low-Flight Training in Italy Near '98 Ski-Lift Deaths
1999-03-11.abstract: Pentagon says US flight crews working temporarily at base in Aviano, Italy, are prohibited from low-level training flights like one that hit ski-gondola cable 13 months ago, killing 20 people (M)

1999-03-11.title: Young Designers Shaped by Dark Gothic of '80s : Minding the (Generational) Gap
1999-03-11.abstract: PARIS—  Jeremy Scott, who will close the latest round of international women's ready-to-wear shows Saturday night, is just one of a number of young designers, with a median age of about 25, currently shaping fashion's future. The dark, gothic aesthetic of his contemporaries, Veronique Branquinho, Olivier Theyskens and Balenciaga's Nicholas Ghesquiere, for example, has had an enormous effect on many a runway this season.

1999-03-12.title: U.N. Criticism Angers Charities Buying Sudan Slaves' Release
1999-03-12.abstract: Private charities that are buying freedom for enslaved children and young women in Sudan react angrily to recent criticism of them from Unicef, United Nations Children's Fund; John Eibner, head of Christian Solidarity International of Zurich, which since 1995 has bought and set free 5,942 Sudanese children, spending about $50 a child, declares 'It is very difficult to understand why Unicef should say our activities are intolerable'; Charles Jacobs of American Anti-Slavery Group in Boston, which helps raise money to support Christian Solidarity, holds 'What is intolerable is to leave these women and children in the hands of brutal captors' (M)

1999-03-12.title: Clinton and Latin Leaders Turn to Immigration
1999-03-12.abstract: Pres Clinton and a group of Central American leaders declare that they will work among themselves and with United States to reach 'humane and permanent solutions' to problems of illegal immigration; declaration is issued at one-day meeting in Antigua, Guatemala, that focuses on reconstruction after a devastating autumn hurricane, development, trade and immigration; photo of Clinton and Latin leaders (M)

1999-03-12.title: Accessory Designers in Britain Cash In on Their Eccentricity : The Extras That Count (and the Zanier the Better)
1999-03-12.abstract: LONDON—  While accessory designers in London may be the unsung heroes of the city's revival as a fashion hotspot, the problem is some of them are victims of their own success.

1999-03-12.title: World Briefing
1999-03-12.abstract: ASIA    INDONESIA: EAST TIMOR BALLOT -- Indonesia and Portugal agreed to let East Timor decide through a direct ballot whether to accept Indonesia's offer of greater autonomy or seek full independence. Details of the vote remain to be worked out. The accord came after two days of talks at the U.N. among the Indonesian Foreign Minister, Ali Alatas, left, the Portuguese Foreign Minister, Jaime Gama, and Secretary General Kofi Annan. Indonesia has still has not decided how much additional autonomy to allow the former Portuguese colony. Further talks will be held next month.    Paul Lewis (NYT)

1999-03-12.title: In Vote Clinton Sought to Avoid, House Backs a Force for Kosovo
1999-03-12.abstract: US House votes narrowly to support Pres Clinton's plan to send American troops to Kosovo should a peace settlement be reached, after a passionate and bitter debate on United States policy in Balkans; 219-to-191 vote reflects House deeply divided over Clinton's efforts to commit troops to another peace settlement in Balkans; supporters of Clinton argue that United States has a moral obligation to stop a genocidal war that could ignite a broader conflict; opponents--including House majority leader and House Republican whip--argue that Kosovo would prove to be a quagmire, and that Europe could police any settlement (M)

1999-03-13.title: There's but One Rule for Footwear Here:Display!: In Japan, the Allure of Feet
1999-03-13.abstract: TOKYO—  When the Japanese talk about female sexuality, the one thing everyone agrees on is the allure of feet. For more than a millennium, women's feet and footwear have played a major role in defining femininity. Fifteenth-century samurai warriors picked concubines by their feet, often without bothering to inspect the rest. In 19th-century Edo (old Tokyo), presenting a woman with sandals was the prelude to any sexual relationship. And until about 30 years ago, Kyoto geishas treated favored clients with the sight of their bare feet.

1999-03-13.title: Oran Journal; Algeria's 'Train of Death' A Bit Safer, God Willing
1999-03-13.abstract: Algerian paramilitary police officers are riding Train 1001 between Algiers and Oran in effort to cut down on attacks by Islamic rebels as it travels through Atlas Mountains; map; photo (M)

1999-03-13.title: Market for Cashmere Shawl Expands : The Lure of Pashmina
1999-03-13.abstract: NEW DELHI—  The Himalayan mountains are a long way from the world's fashion capitols, but this remote region at the top of the world is the source of one of this season's best-selling accessories. Brilliantly colored, featherweight pashmina shawls have become a fashion sensation, combining today's essential elements of style: comfort and luxury.

1999-03-13.title: At NATO Event, an Italian Call for Justice
1999-03-13.abstract: Italian Prime Minister Massimo D'Alema, in Poland to mark expansion of NATO, expresses outrage that US Marine Corps Capt Richard J Ashby was acquitted of charges connected to flying jet into ski gondola cable in northern Italy, killing 20 people; says that he expects US to find those who are responsible for disaster, and if not, Italy might have to reconsider how jurisdiction is awarded in future (M)

1999-03-13.title: World Briefing
1999-03-13.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: JEHOVAH'S WITNESS RULING -- Final judgment was deferred in a closely-watched court case against the Jehovah's Witnesses, who have been charged with violating Russia's restrictive law on religion. A judge sent the case to a panel that includes two linguists, a psychologist and two experts on religion. Rights advocates, who see the case as a test of religious tolerance, said the order was an attempt to put off a politically-charged decision.    Celestine Bohlen (NYT)

1999-03-14.title: Bombs Kill 6 in Kosovo Markets As Leaders Depart for Paris Talks
1999-03-14.abstract: Three powerful bombs exloded in midst of markets in two towns in Kosovo, killing at least six people, including two children, and wounding dozens more; attack comes as leaders of ethnic Albanian majority leave for new round of peace talks with Serbs in Paris; two sides blame each other (M)

1999-03-14.title: U.S. General Recommends Pullout From Haiti
1999-03-14.abstract: Gen Charles E Wilhelm, commander of US forces in Latin America and the Caribbean, says American troops in Haiti are being put in danger by country's increasing political turbulence and should be removed; recommends periodic visits by US military personnel (M)

1999-03-14.title: In New Talks on Kosovo, NATO's Credibility Is at Stake
1999-03-14.abstract: NATO leaders believe that alliance's credibility is at stake in coming peace talks on Kosovo in Paris; delegation representing Albanian rebels in Kosovo have still not signed internatinal peace plan for autonomy worked out in February, and Pres Slobodan Milosevic of Yugoslavia has not budged from rejection of number of appeals by European, American and Russian officials to agree to take the peacekeepers provided under the agreement; photo (M)

1999-03-14.title: Bolivian's Dark Past Starts to Catch Up With Him
1999-03-14.abstract: Hugo Banzer was democratically elected President of Bolivia in 1997, two decades after his military dictatorship; disappearance of 200 Bolivians during Banzer's rule from 1971 to 1978 was only minor issue in election campaign; Banzer's fortunes began to change following arrest of Gen Augusto Pinochet of Chile in London on Spanish judge's charge that he led effort to kidnap and kill leftists in five South American countries in 1970's; Banzer is being besieged by allegations from local human rights groups, press and even senior congressional members of his ruling coalition that he knowingly participated in joint operations with Chilean and Argentine military Governments as part of larger regional effort called Operation Condor that led to deaths of at least 36 people; map; photos (M)

1999-03-14.title: Russia Remains Uneasy Over NATO's Expansion
1999-03-14.abstract: NATO's gala 50th anniversary celebration in Washington may or may not include Russia, which remains uneasy about alliance, an old enemy that now includes former Soviet bloc countries of Poland, Czech Republic and Hungary; NATO has confidentially promised to limit crisis deployments to three countries, but Russian defense figure Sergei Karaganov says his country's strategy is still to have NATO as 'weak' as possible because 'we are not part of it'; Russia's longterm strategy is also to discourage NATO from trying to include Baltic states or other former Soviet-bloc nations, by making process as diplomatically painful as possible; anxiety is preoccupation of Russian elite and ranks low on average citizen's long litany of woes; polling graph (M)

1999-03-15.title: Villagers Hope Kosovo Peace Arrives Before the War
1999-03-15.abstract: Novo Selo, Serbia, one of few mixed villages left in Kosovo, is place where Serbs and ethnic Albanians live side by side in apparent harmony; they are surrounded by violence, and as conflict escalates around them, they cling to hope that peace will reach them before war does; photo; map (M)

1999-03-15.title: William E. Warwick, Commodore of QE2, Dies at 86
1999-03-15.abstract: Commodore William E Warwick, Cunard mariner who became first master of last great ocean liner Queen Elizabeth 2, dies at age 86; photo (M)

1999-03-15.title: Will Beijing's Nuclear Arsenal Stay Small or Will It Mushroom?
1999-03-15.abstract: Washington questions whether China's current bare-bones arsenal of missiles and nuclear weapons will grow to become more potent arsenal that could rekindle kind of fears that shaped cold war; suspicion that China stole design of America's most advanced miniaturized warhead from Los Alamos National Laboratory 10 years ago has prompted anger in Washington, especially in Congress; despite continuing evidence of Chinese espionage abroad, most experts doubt China intends to change fundamentally its largely defensive nuclear strategy; experts outside US Government say China wants arsenal large enough to give it global status and deter potential for nuclear blackmail, but small enough so that country will not be bankrupted by its military force, as Russia was; they say China's modernization could accelerate its ability to threaten its neighbors; map (M)

1999-03-15.title: 'No Question,' U.S. Says, Leak Helped China
1999-03-15.abstract: Pres Clinton's national security adviser, Samuel R Berger, says there is no question that China benefited from important information leaked from Los Alamos National Laboratory; Republicans and some Democrats are calling for further investigation into whether Administration delayed investigating loss after first suspicions arose in 1996 (M)

1999-03-15.title: Capitalists to Be Granted Official Status in Communist China
1999-03-15.abstract: China's Parliament will amend Constitution to declare private business 'important component of the socialist market economy,' and it will further amend Constitution to incorporate rule of law; to China's fast-growing business class, change brings new sense of legitimacy and protection in country where business owners ae still likely to face significant political, economic and social discrimination; at practical level, business executives expect change to help end vast array of discriminatory practices that make it hard for them to buy land or get loans (M)

1999-03-16.title: Ethnic Albanians Move to Accept Kosovo Pact
1999-03-16.abstract: Veton Surroi, leader of ethnic Albanian delegation in Paris, says it is ready to sign Kosovo peace accord; French officials say signing was delayed while mediators consulted Serbs to see whether they would accept agreement with some minor changes; Serbs have accepted most of political provisions, but have refused to consider NATO peacekeepers to enforce it (M)

1999-03-16.title: Banks in Ecuador Reopen After Week's Closing, but Taxi Strike Aggravates Tensions
1999-03-16.abstract: Banks in Ecuador open for first time in over week, during which Government had kept them closed because of worsening financial crisis; strike by taxi drivers blocks roads and brings commerce and transport to near halt; taxi drivers are protesting tripling in gasoline prices, to over $3 gallon, announced by Pres Jamil Mahuad (M)

1999-03-16.title: Los Alamos Scientist Admits Contacts With Chinese, U.S. Says
1999-03-16.abstract: Senior intelligence and law enforcement officials say Wen Ho Lee, Taiwan-born scientist at Los Alamos National Laboratory questioned in nuclear espionage investigation, admitted to Federal Bureau of Investigation that he had unauthorized contacts with Chinese scientists; say his admissions helped cement decision by Energy Sec Bill Richardson to fire him on March 8; say Lee's statements had not provided enough evidence to lead to his arrest (M)

1999-03-16.title: LEADING SENATORS DEMAND U.S. LIMIT HELP FOR BEIJING
1999-03-16.abstract: Sens Jesse Helms and Ernest F Hollings, reflecting Congressional anger over Pres Clinton's response to suspicions that China stole nuclear secrets, threaten to block Administration's efforts to help China become World Trade Organization member this year; also urge suspension of some scientific exchange programs; Sen Richard C Shelby urges Clinton or Energy Sec Bill Richardson to put moratorium on visits by scientists from countries like China to US national weapons laboratories, and on reciprocal visits by US scientists to foreign installations; CIA Dir George Tenet says retired Adm David E Jeremiah will head independent panel of experts to review possible harm to national security resulting from suspected thefts that took place in 1980's and were discovered by nuclear arms experts at Los Alamos National Laboratory in 1995 (M)

1999-03-16.title: Panel Says Smallpox Stocks May Be Useful
1999-03-16.abstract: Institute of Medicine report finds that preserving last known samples of deadly smallpox virus in Russia and US may provide important scientific and medical opportunities that would be lost if virus is destroyed this year, as planned; report is likely to influence Pres Clinton's thinking about politically and scientifically contentious issue (M)

1999-03-17.title: Trademark Traits Bring Cresson's Career to Close
1999-03-17.abstract: PARIS—  Taking up a sought-after appointment on the European Commission in Brussels four years ago, Edith Cresson seemed to put a safe distance between herself and her image from early in the decade as a headstrong politician with a knack for insulting remarks, including slurs against Britain and Japan when she was France's first female prime minister.

1999-03-17.title: Mouse That Roared: European Parliament Stands Up
1999-03-17.abstract: News analysis of abrupt resignation of entire executive leadership of 15-member European Union amid charges of fraud and gross mismanagement; resignation clearly reflects deeper malaise in which democratic accountability of organization's Brussels-based leadership has not grown, even though size and power of united Europe have expanded; underscores need for European leaders to pay greater attention to theme of democracy (M)

1999-03-17.title: Nuclear Lapses Known in '96, Aides Now Say
1999-03-17.abstract: Senior Clinton Administration aide ordered late in 1996 that security measures at nation's nuclear weapons laboratories be quickly strengthened, months earlier than previously acknowledged, but orders were largely ignored or delayed; Deputy Energy Sec Charles B Curtis ordered that series of security measures be carried out on strict timetable, but measures were not taken despite warning in department's annual security review for year that department was at greater risk of becoming victim of espionage than at any time in its history; officials say lapses occurred mostly during transitional period, during which Federico Pena became Energy Sec in 1997 and Curtis left office month later (M)

1999-03-17.title: Mitteleuropa Loses a Voice As Soros Shuts His Magazine
1999-03-17.abstract: Transitions, post-Communist world's sole magazine devoted to analyzing region's own transformations, ceases publication--victim of Russian financial crisis, bad management and shifting priorities of financier George Soros, its main supporter (M)

1999-03-17.title: Sterkfontein Journal; Solving a 3.5-Million-Year-Old, 3-D Jigsaw Puzzle
1999-03-17.abstract: Interview with Dr Ronald J Clarke, South African anthropologist engaged in effort to extract skeleton believed to be 3.5 million years old from Sterkfontein caves near Johannesburg; his discovery, announced in December 1998, may help answer some enduring questions about human evolution; scientists have likened find to discovery of famous 'Lucy' skeleton in Ethiopia in 1974; photo; maps (M)

1999-03-18.title: Balloon Crew Has First Global Nonstop in Sight
1999-03-18.abstract: Breitling Orbiter 3, having floated some 20,000 miles over Europe, Africa, Asia, Pacific Ocean and Central America, heads into home stretch over Atlantic Ocean in its bid to become first balloon to circle world nonstop; Dr Bertrand Piccard is pilot and Brian Jones is co-pilot; diagram (M)

1999-03-18.title: American Topics : Tattoos Guide Campus Nurses
1999-03-18.abstract: It is a blustery day at Rutgers University, in New Jersey, and Walter Hewitt, a nurse at the health center, is treating his 10th cold of the day. "Open your mouth," he says to a student ... and out pops a silver tongue ring. Down the hall, when a nurse practitioner, Judith Greif, asks another student to lift his shirt for an examination, she notices his nipples are pierced.

1999-03-18.title: Israeli Politician Convicted of Corruption
1999-03-18.abstract: Aryeh Deri, charismatic Israeli politician, is convicted on corruption charges in verdict that outrages ultra-Orthodox Sephardic Jews who consider him champion of their community; photo (M)

1999-03-18.title: Europeans Focus on Local Content to Spur Internet Growth
1999-03-18.abstract: LONDON—  One of the biggest challenges to the growth of the Internet in Europe can be summed up in a few words. Words like Yahoo!, AOL or Amazon.com.

1999-03-18.title: 2 Late Israeli Leaders, 2 Grand Museums
1999-03-18.abstract: Families and allies of Yitzhak Rabin and Menachem Begin are raising millions of dollars to build museum-scale centers to honor late Israeli Prime Ministers (M)

1999-03-19.title: Doubts on NATO Air Raids As the Talks on Kosovo End
1999-03-19.abstract: Clinton Administration, with Kosovo talks at a dead end, and Yugoslav leaders more recalcitrant than ever, is publicly pushing threat of air strikes, but officials say they have no option but diplomacy, at least for another week; Administration's policy of using threat of air strikes to force Pres Slobodan Milosevic to sign peace agreement has failed so far; peace talks conclude in Paris with ethnic Albanians signing an agreement on autonomy, but Serbs still refusing to accept it (M)

1999-03-19.title: Saudis Release Iraqi Plane in Sanctions Dispute
1999-03-19.abstract: Saudi Arabia releases an Iraqi plane it seized on Wednesday after United Nations sanctions committee failed to agree on whether such flights of religious pilgrims violate United Nations sanctions; UN officials say that plane that Saudi authorities detained at Jidda airport was carrying 111 Iraqis traveling to Mecca for the haj pilgrimage to Mohamed's birthplace required once in a lifetime of all pious Muslims who can afford it (M)

1999-03-19.title: Clinton Asks Panel to Analyze and Solve Security Threats to Nuclear Labs
1999-03-19.abstract: Pres Clinton asks an advisory panel today to analyze security threats at Energy Department's nuclear laboratories, after suspicions surfaced that China stole nuclear-weapons designs from United States (S)

1999-03-19.title: A. Doak Barnett Dies; China Scholar, 77
1999-03-19.abstract: Arthur Doak Barnett, leading scholar and government adviser on China, dies at age 77; photo (M)

1999-03-19.title: World Briefing
1999-03-19.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: 'GENETIC FOOD' WARNING -- Britain ordered restaurants, cafes, delicatessens, fast-food outlets and caterers to tell their customers if their meals contain genetically modified products. Failure to comply with the regulations, which go into effect immediately, could bring fines of up to $8,750.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

1999-03-20.title: Monitors and Embassy Staff Told to Leave Yugoslavia
1999-03-20.abstract: International monitors are ordered to pull out of Kosovo, and Western embassies order their nonessential staff to leave country after peace talks collapse in Paris; NATO officials say no bombing will be made until all cease-fire monitors are safety out (M)

1999-03-20.title: New-Generation, but Noisy, Australian Submarines Under Attack
1999-03-20.abstract: SYDNEY—  It was touted as the most advanced nonnuclear submarine in the world — a silent, potent leviathan capable of staying submerged for 70 days and patrolling over long distances.

1999-03-20.title: '400 Years' in Irish Jail Lasts Just 16 Months
1999-03-20.abstract: Four Irish Republican Army guerrillas convicted of terrorist crimes in Northern Ireland are sentenced to a total of 400 years in prison, but they will be freed by July 2000 under terms of peace agreement; about 300 Catholic IRA and Protestant paramilitary prisoners have already been released early under agreement (S)

1999-03-20.title: Jean Pierre-Bloch, 93, Is Dead; Resistance Fighter for France
1999-03-20.abstract: Jean Pierre-Bloch, French resistance figure, high-ranking member of de Gaulle's Free French movement during World War II, dies at 93; photo (M)

1999-03-20.title: Clinton Says Force Is Needed to Halt Kosovo Bloodshed
1999-03-20.abstract: Pres Clinton says that Serbs have crossed the threshold of acceptable behavior and that force is the answer to prevent further bloodshed in Kosovo; says that beyond human toll, conflict in Kosovo could send tens of thousands of refugees across borders and possibly draw Albania, Macedonia, Greece and Turkey into the war; makes case to Congressional leaders, but they remain skeptical about the reasons for US involvement in hostilities in former Yugoslavia; Western Governments begin removing cease-fire monitors and nonessential personnel from Yugoslavia; Pentagon dispatches additional combat aircraft to region; Clinton photo (M)

1999-03-21.title: Balloon History, and in Only 20 Days
1999-03-21.abstract: Dr Bertrand Piccard and Brian Jones sail into history when their Breitling Orbiter 3 completes the first nonstop balloon trip around world; balloon, at altitude of 36,000 feet, sped past finish line at at 9 degrees 27 minutes west longitude over Mauritania; harrowing trip had taken pilots over Europe, Africa, Asia, Pacific Ocean, Central America and Atlantic Ocean; photo; map (L)

1999-03-21.title: Ruling Says Chirac Can't Be Prosecuted on Corruption Charge
1999-03-21.abstract: French prosecutor Yves Bot rules that French President Jacques Chirac cannot be prosecuted while in office on political corruption charges stemming from his years as Mayor of Paris and leader of his conservative Gaullist party (M)

1999-03-21.title: Fears Deepens as Monitors Quit Kosovo
1999-03-21.abstract: Thousands of panicked refugees flee their homes and villages as Serbian forces press an offensive against ethnic Albanian rebels in Kosovo; offensive comes as foreign monitors are evacuated from Kosovo after peace talks for Serbian province collapse and liklihood of NATO air strikes grows; photo (M)

1999-03-21.title: Russian With Many Faces May Get Lift in U.S. Visit
1999-03-21.abstract: Russian Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov is facing one of his greatest challenges this week when he visits United States in effort to win several billions of dollars in credits from skeptical International Monetary Fund; success would help Russia to regain modicum of respectability on world financial markets as well as to restructure its colossal foreign debt; it would also make Primakov a conquering hero at home, with greatly improved chances of succeeding President Boris Yeltsin; photo (M)

1999-03-21.title: Canada Faces New Pressure To Safeguard Rare Wildlife
1999-03-21.abstract: Wildlife advocates are pressuring Canadian Government to adopt national endangered species law to protect wildlife and its habitats; some US wildlife groups plan to institute legal action that raises threat of trade sanctions against Canada unless effective law is passed (M)

1999-03-22.title: With Balloon's Work Done, Its Builder Reveals a Secret
1999-03-22.abstract: Builders of Breitling Orbiter 3 balloon, in which Dr Bertrand Piccard and Brian Jones became first to circumnavigate world in balloon, disclose some technical innovations that led to success; says experience gained from previous flights was important factor; cites use of foam insulation around helium-filled central section that kept gas from expanding and leaking out during day and from cooling and contracting too much at night; photo (M)

1999-03-22.title: Irish Premier Sees Progress On Arms Issue In the North
1999-03-22.abstract: Irish Prime Min Bertie Ahern says there will be settlement of dispute over Irish Republican Army disarmament in time for Northern Ireland peace effort to advance by Easter deadline; will meet soon with Northern Ireland political leaders; notes positive indications from Gerry Adams of Irish Republican Army and David Trimble, Protestant First Minister of Northern Ireland Assembly (M)

1999-03-22.title: MILOSEVIC TO GET ONE 'LAST CHANCE' TO AVOID BOMBING
1999-03-22.abstract: Clinton Administration dispatches special envoy Richard C Holbrooke to meet with Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic in what officials say it last chance to persuade him to accept peace agreement for Kosovo and avoid bombing by NATO; Sec of State Madeleine K Albright says Milosevic will be presented with 'stark choice' of either agreeing to settlement signed in Paris or face NATO air strikes; US officials at NATO headquarters in Brussels present NATO ambassadors with stepped-up plan for bombing in move intended to strengthen Holbrooke's position with Milosevic; national security adviser Samuel R Berger says another diplomatic push by Holbrooke is justified one final time, even as Administration comes under criticism for handling of Milosevic; Serbian forces seem unintimidated; take advantage of withdrawal of more than 1,300 international monitors to continue rampage through Kosovo, burning ethnic Albanian villages in region at heart of conflict (M)

1999-03-22.title: Saudis Admit Restricting U.S. Warplanes in Iraq
1999-03-22.abstract: Nearly three months of US bombings against Iraq are proving irritant to ties between US and Saudi Arabia; senior Saudi officials acknowledge kingdom refuses to allow American warplanes based in Riyadh to take part in what it perceives as punitive raids; stand has forced US to rely more heavily on navy aircraft and on warplanes at other bases; while Saudi officials make clear privately that they would prefer someone other than Saddam Hussein ruling Iraq, their military position is indication of discomfort felt over American-led campaign (M)

1999-03-22.title: Serbs Burn the Birthplace Of the Albanians' Revolt
1999-03-22.abstract: Serbian troops burn and destroy village of Prekaz, birthplace of armed Albanian revolt against Serbia's rule of Kosovo; refugees flee region; fighting reported in nearby village of Poljance as Serb forces take advantage of withdrawal of international monitors to launch attacks; Serbian tanks and heavy concentrations of troops surround Drenica; four Serbian policemen reportedly die in hail of gunfire from passing car in capital of Pristina; police patrol reportedly ambushed near town of Suva Reka; maps; photos (M)

1999-03-23.title: U.S. ISSUES APPEAL TO SERBS TO HALT ATTACK IN KOSOVO
1999-03-23.abstract: Special US envoy Richard C Holbrooke, under orders not to let Serbia delay NATO action while Kosovo burns, meets with Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic in Belgrade in last-ditch effort to negotiate at least cease-fire; says little was achieved in more than three hours of talks with Milosevic; says heightened military activities in Kosovo make it extremely difficult to pursue peace; senior NATO official says Serbian forces have destroyed three of Kosovo Liberation Army's seven reigonal headquarters in past three days, adding to urgency of Holbrooke mission; Serb forces burn villages and drive civilians from homes in Kosovo, whose population is predominantly ethnic Albanians; Pres Clinton warns military action could be imminent if Milosevic continues to choose agression over peace; says 30,000 more Albanians have been displaced from homes in last few days; says Milosevic will not be allowed to continue aggression with impunity since America's security interests must include Europe's freedom and security; photo (M)

1999-03-23.title: Senators Clash Over U.S. Role In a NATO Bombing Campaign
1999-03-23.abstract: Handful of US Senators clash over American role in NATO air campaign if Belgrade refuses to sign peace accord with ethnic Albanians in Kosovo province (S)

1999-03-23.title: U.N. Warns U.S. to Pay Bill or Lose Assembly Vote
1999-03-23.abstract: UN warns US must pay at least $250 million in back dues in 1999 or lose its vote in General Assembly; figure supersedes more than $197 paid toward end of 1998 to avoid losing Assembly vote in 1999 (S)

1999-03-23.title: Top Ethnic Albanian Rebel Asks NATO to Start Strikes
1999-03-23.abstract: Suleyman Selimi, general commander of Kosovo Liberation Army, calls on NATO to carry out air strikes on Serbia to put end to violence, as Yugoslav Army and police forces continue ransacking and burning villages; special Serbian police units continue setting houses and farm buildings ablaze in village of Poljance; maps; photos (M)

1999-03-23.title: Borneo Death March /Of 2,700 Prisoners, 6 Survived : An Old Soldier Remembers a Wartime Atrocity
1999-03-23.abstract: SANDAKAN, Malaysia—  Owen Campbell returned to Borneo last week, back to the jungles where half a century ago his best mates were marched to their deaths.

1999-03-24.title: Yeltsin's Inner Circle Under Investigation for Corruption
1999-03-24.abstract: Russia's chief prosecutor's office confirms that it is investigating corruption inside Kremlin, aimed at members of Pres Boris N Yeltsin's inner circle; investigators, acting on orders from general prosecutor Yuri I Skuratov, have seized documents from offices of presidential administration's powerful property management office, headed by Pavel P Borodin; Skuratov recently defied Kremlin order to resign; photo (M)

1999-03-24.title: Sir Michael Caine, 71, Muse Behind Britain's Booker Prize
1999-03-24.abstract: Sir Michael Caine, corporate chief with love of books who was driving force behind Booker Prize for Fiction, Britain's most prestigious literary award, dies at age 71; photo (M)

1999-03-24.title: U.S. Negotiators Depart, Frustrated by Milosevic's Hard Line
1999-03-24.abstract: Senior US official says that Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic refused 'every opportunity' to avoid onslaught of NATO air power during negotiations with US envoy Richard C Holbrooke (M)

1999-03-24.title: A Wary Senate Gives Support For Air Strikes
1999-03-24.abstract: Senate votes, 58 to 41, to support NATO air strikes against Serbian military targets; vote approves one-sentence resolution authorizing Pres Clinton to carry out US bombing and missile attacks as part of NATO's efforts to end crisis in Kosovo (M)

1999-03-24.title: Malaysian Trial Over Sex and Graft Ends Abruptly; Verdict to Come Later
1999-03-24.abstract: Politically charged sex-and-corruption trial of Malaysia's former Deputy Prime Min Anwar Ibrahim comes to sudden end when Judge Anthony Paul refuses to hear demand by defense that he step down because of pro-prosecution bias; Paul says he will announce verdict in two weeks (M)

1999-03-25.title: Anti-Apartheid Cleric Sentenced to 6 Years for Embezzlement
1999-03-25.abstract: South African Judge John Foxcroft sentences former anti-apartheid activist Allan Boesak to six years in prison for stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars donated to help needy children and educate black voters (M)

1999-03-25.title: MORE ON THE BOMBINGS
1999-03-25.abstract: Clinton Explains Decision  The President said he decided to use force because of moral revulsion at the killings in Kosovo and to serve American interests. Page A15.    Serbs Turn Back to Loyalty  Even the Serbs most bitterly opposed to President Slobodan Milosevic feel a surge of nationalism at a threat from the West. Page A14.

1999-03-25.title: After the Years of Mourning, a Time for Joy
1999-03-25.abstract: Albanian-Americans in New York metropolitan area rejoice when Pres Clinton announces that NATO bombing of Serbia had begun, but their joy is mixed with fear for their relatives and US servicemen; photo (M)

1999-03-25.title: War Minus Pictures Puts Crimp In Reports
1999-03-25.abstract: Caryn James article on television coverage of NATO bombing of Serbia; says after Yugoslav authorities shut down site that Western news organizations had used to transmit their stories, viewers were tossed back to pre-satellite era of television; says reporters in Belgrade and Pristina talked on phone while television screen showed still photograph of that correspondent alongside map marked with his or her location (M)

1999-03-25.title: In 2001, I'll See You on the Phone
1999-03-25.abstract: EXISTING mobile phones have not allowed users to make calls in the United States, Europe and Asia using the same phone. But the next generation of mobile networks will allow them to do so and will provide better services to boot.

1999-03-26.title: Loud Knocks In the Night, And Orders To Get Out
1999-03-26.abstract: About 50 Western journalists flee after Serbian Government orders all members of news media from NATO countries to leave; some take refuge in Skopje, Macedonia; some journalists and crews are roughed up; while atmosphere in Pristina, capital of Kosovo province, is reportedly more tense than elsewhere in country, other journalists also face harassment; two American reporters are held in Belgrade police station for 11 hours (M)

1999-03-26.title: Russia Takes Protest to the U.N. Council
1999-03-26.abstract: Russia introduces Security Council resolution demanding immediate end to NATO's air strikes against Yugoslavia, denouncing them as 'threat to international peace' and 'flagrant violation' of both United Nations Charter and Yugoslav sovereignty (S)

1999-03-26.title: China Mounts All-Out Verbal Assault on Air Raids
1999-03-26.abstract: China presses an all-out verbal assault on NATO air strikes against Serbian forces, issuing multiple demands that attack be halted, while flagship state newspaper called operation a 'dangerous precedent of naked aggression' (S)

1999-03-26.title: The NATO General Who Is Intimately Familiar With His Adversary
1999-03-26.abstract: Profile of Wesley K Clark, four-star American general who as Supreme Allied Commander in Europe is in charge of powerful NATO air offensive against Serbia for its attacks against ethnic Albanians in Kosovo; an allied diplomat, not American, says he thinks that Clark, 54, is intellectually the most impressive chief alliance has ever had, but that he is fixated on idea that force is the only language Serb leadership understands (M)

1999-03-26.title: Fight Over Fish Poaching Stirs Up Icy Southern Sea
1999-03-26.abstract: MELBOURNE—  Bitterly cold, terrifyingly remote and frequently lashed by gale force winds and mountainous waves, the Southern Ocean seems an unlikely Eldorado for poachers.

1999-03-27.title: Milosevic Might Win From the Loss of Kosovo
1999-03-27.abstract: Whispers about a face-saving Serbian loss to the world's most powerful military alliance began here long before NATO bombs started to fall on Yugoslavia.    The whispers -- reinforced earlier this week in interviews with well-placed officials in the regime of Slobodan Milosevic, with opposition leaders and with Belgrade residents -- all follow a similar line of Balkan logic. It goes like this:

1999-03-27.title: Russia Cancels Lewinsky Visit
1999-03-27.abstract: Russia's rage over NATO air strikes in Yugoslavia boiled over tonight when it canceled the upcoming visit by Monica Lewinsky.    The former White House intern had been scheduled to visit Russia in June or July to promote a Russian-language version of her book, ''Monica's Story.''

1999-03-27.title: White House Tells of Reports Of a Forced March in Kosovo
1999-03-27.abstract: Clinton Adm and Orgn for Security and Cooperation in Europe say that they have received reports from refugees fleeing Kosovo and others that estimated 15,000 to 20,000 ethnic Albanian civilians were put on forced march in central Kosovo by Serbian forces; Adm cautions that it does not have satellite imagery to confirm accounts; but State Dept says it will monitor 'unfolding events in Kosovo' and collect evidence of any war crimes, like summary executions (M)

1999-03-27.title: Reports of Reprisals Rely on Refugee Accounts and Scratchy Phone Calls
1999-03-27.abstract: On a rocky hill that overlooks the village in Kosovo 20 yards across the border that he fled last week, Agim, 27, squinted through binoculars today at two Serbian armored vehicles parked in the middle of his town.     ''I've seen soldiers looting televisions and refrigerators,'' he reported. ''They chased us all away, and now they are stealing everything.''

1999-03-27.title: Russia's Move To End Strikes Loses; Margin Is a Surprise
1999-03-27.abstract: The United States and the NATO alliance won a victory today when  the Security Council rejected, 12 to 3,  a Russian resolution demanding an immediate end to NATO's air attacks on Yugoslavia.    Voting in favor of the measure were two of its sponsors, Russia and China, and Namibia. Voting against were the five Council members who are NATO members -- the United States, Britain, France, Canada and the Netherlands -- together with Argentina, Bahrain, Brazil, Gabon, Gambia, Malaysia and Slovenia.

1999-03-28.title: U.N. to Create Own Satellite Program to Find Illegal Drug Crops
1999-03-28.abstract: United Nations Commission on Narcotic Drugs, meeting, Vienna, gives its drug control program unanimous approval to create own satellite monitoring system to find drug crops in major source nations; Pino Arlacchi, program head, says European Space Agency will provide satellites and technical expertise, phone interview; says European Commission will pay part of cost, estimated at as little as $15 million a year (M)

1999-03-28.title: In Renault-Nissan Deal, Big Risks and Big Opportunities
1999-03-28.abstract: Renault's deal to take stake in troubled Nissan Motor Co seen risky business gamble to strengthen global competitiveness of both companies; tries to balance differing needs of Japanese auto company and Western one; helps resolve Nissan's financial problems while allowing its management to save face; seeks to insulate Renault from Nissan's financial woes, while giving it access to Nissan's advanced engineering and technical skills and car making capacity in United States and Mexico; deal may raise as many challenges as it seeks to resolve; photo (M)

1999-03-28.title: Good Friends Join Enemies To Criticize U.S. on Rights
1999-03-28.abstract: United States, which considers itself bastion of human rights, comes under attack from friend and foe alike at United Nations annual meeting on global democratic rights, Geneva; Amnesty International places US for first time on its list of human rights violators; key issue is death penalty (M)

1999-03-28.title: Myanmar Opposition Leader's Husband Dies, Denied a Last Visit
1999-03-28.abstract: Michael Aris, 53, British husband of Burmese opposition leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, dies of cancer in London after being barred by Myanmar authorities from visiting her; she has refused to leave Myanmar, fearing authorities would bar her return; American Embassy in Myanmar, in unusual move, phones her message of thanks to those who helped Aris to reporters abroad (M)

1999-03-28.title: Old Enmities Puncture Baseball Detente in Cuba
1999-03-28.abstract: Baltimore Orioles are set to play exhibition game in Havana against team of Cuban all-stars during particularly chilly period in cold war relationship between Cuba and United States; Cuba is target of international condemnation over its new curbs on growing internal dissent; photo (M)

1999-03-29.title: CONFLICT IN THE BALKANS: IN ALBANIA -- NATO Planes Step Up Attacks on Serb Troops; Long Lines of Refugees Hounded Into Albania
1999-03-29.abstract: Tens of thousands of ethnic Albanians, driven from Kosovo villages by Serbian forces, flock into neighboring Albania, threatening to overwhelm country's scant resources and hasty efforts of Western aid workers; tell harrowing tales of brutality by Serbian police and paramilitary units that appear to show deliberate effort by Serbs to apply tactics of 'ethnic cleansing' used in Bosnia; photos; map (M)

1999-03-29.title: Cairo Journal; When Sheep Are Invited, Much to Their Dismay
1999-03-29.abstract: Countless sheep are ritually slaughtered in Cairo, Egypt, at start of Id al-Lahma, or Muslim feast of sacrifice, which is one of most important festivals on Islamic calender that commemmorates Ibrahim's willingness to sacrifice his son in obedience to God; ritual, mandatory only for pilgrims in Mecca but urged on all Muslims who can afford it, described; photos (M)

1999-03-29.title: CONFLICT IN THE BALKANS: IN BELGRADE -- NATO Planes Step Up Attacks on Serb Troops; Support for Homeland Up as Sirens Wail and News Is Censored
1999-03-29.abstract: Slobodan Milosevic, defiant as ever and proud of his nation's resistance to NATO's air attacks, staged an eerie celebration today -- a rock concert in the center of Belgrade to show solidarity with the soldiers facing down the allied assaults.    Few of the thousands of Serbs who attended were unaware that this was also the 10th anniversary of the day when Mr. Milosevic stripped broad autonomy from Kosovo, just after his security forces had killed some 30 ethnic Albanian protesters in Pristina, Kosovo's capital.

1999-03-29.title: Balkan Rarity: A Peaceful Mixture of Ethnic Groups, All Looking to the West
1999-03-29.abstract: Macedonia is only former Yugoslav republic that has peacefully retained ethnically mixed society, but war being waged at its borders in Kosovo is testing country's ability to keep itself intact; working in its favor is ambition to move toward Western-style democracy and widely shared dread of ending up like neighbors; photo (M)

1999-03-29.title: Tactics Were Barrier To Top Serb's Indictment
1999-03-29.abstract: Although US officials consider Slobodan Milosevic largely responsible for much of warring that has ravaged Balkans for past eight years, he has not been indicted by international war crimes tribunal, publicly or secretly; American and tribunal officials say US Government and tribunal are responsible for Milosevic's avoidance of legal accountability; Clinton Administration made him partner in seeking peace in Bosnia, and neither court nor US has considered indictment priority; neither have devoted resources to amassing evidence for indictment (M)

1999-03-30.title: 2 Mexican Banks to Plead Guilty in Laundering Case
1999-03-30.abstract: Grupo Financiero Bancomer SA and Grupo Financiero Serfin SA, two of Mexico's biggest banks, will plead guilty to criminal charges of money laundering in move bringing swift end to most important legal cases stemming from major investigation into movement of illegal drug profits; will forfeit millions of dollars seized by Govt and pay fines of $500,000 each; outcome is triumph for group of Federal prosecutors, Justice Dept officials and US Customs Service who resisted strong pressure from both Mexican Govt and banks to have charges dropped (M)

1999-03-30.title: Command, Control and Adrenaline, and Relief at Pilot's Rescue
1999-03-30.abstract: Interview with combat crew of Lockheed EC-130E command-and-control aircraft that herds NATO warplanes flying bombing attacks over Yugoslavia; diagram (M)

1999-03-30.title: Could a Small Ground Force Carve Out Safe Havens?
1999-03-30.abstract: Several current and former American commanders say if NATO ordered ground troops to Kosovo, vanguard of force of 30,000 to 40,000 troops could be assembled in days and would be sufficient to protect many refugees from further attack; say small number of troops, backed by air power, could sweep into besieged province and create havens for tens of thousands of ethnic Albanians fleeing Serbian attacks (M)

1999-03-30.title: Milosevic Goal Is Serb Enclave, NATO Declares
1999-03-30.abstract: NATO officials say Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic's goal in Kosovo is to carve out Serb-only enclave in province that he can keep after fighting ends; say campaign is under way to ethnically re-engineer Kosovo to achieve solution more acceptable to him; say Milosevic has proven adept over past eight years at creating 'facts on the ground'--territorial conquests that end in cease-fires and are presented to West for ratification through peace deal; concerns are heightened by visit of Russian Prime Min Yevgeny Primakov and Foreign and Defense Ministers to Belgrade; NATO officials fear Russians may try to broker agreement well short of allies' stated goal of autonomous Kosovo protected by NATO troops; photo; map (M)

1999-03-30.title: NATO HUNTING FOR SERB FORCES; U.S. REPORTS SIGNS OF 'GENOCIDE'
1999-03-30.abstract: NATO warplanes hunt for Serbian troops in embattled province of Kosovo as Clinton Administration claims evidence of genocide is growing to include abhorrent and criminal action on vast scale; language is strongest yet by State Dept in denouncing Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic; reports persist of assassinations of ethnic Albanian leaders and of scorched-earth torching of Kosovo's villages by Serbian forces; tens of thousands of refugees continue fleeing in terror; pour into Albania, Macedonia and Montenegro, compounding region's profound refugee problem; military officials acknowledge hunt for Serbian forces is complicated by fact that they are operating in small and dispersed groups; new attacks are aimed at barracks, headquarters and other bases, rather than tanks, artillery pieces and small mobile infantry units deployed in field against Kosovo villages; more US warplanes are earmarked for NATO operation, including bombers carrying antitank bombs and aircraft designed to confuse enemy radar systems; Belgrade claims forces shot down second NATO warplane; claim is denied by Pentagon; officials continue withholding information on what brought down F-117 stealth fighter over Yugoslavia days ago; map; photo (M)

1999-03-31.title: Kosovars Flee to Beat Serb Deadline of Death
1999-03-31.abstract: Ethnic Albanians in Kosovo's Has district flee toward Albania after Serbian forces order them to leave or be killed; officials estimate that about 10,800 refugees crossed border by 8 AM on March 30 and that they are coming at rate of 2,000 hour; they join 60,000 ethnic Albanians who fled into Albania day before; photo (M)

1999-03-31.title: After Kosovo's 'Cleansing': Can Two Sides Ever Coexist?
1999-03-31.abstract: Growing refugee crisis in Kosovo has left Clinton Administration and NATO officials wondering whether they will be able to achieve their goal of peaceful settlement between Serbs and ethnic Albanians; nearly a third of 1.8 million ethnic Albanians in Kosovo have been forced from their homes by Serbian troops; many refugees have fled to Macedonia, Albania or Montenegro; State Department spokesman James P Rubin says Administration stands by framework of Rambouillet accord, which calls for Kosovo to stay in Serbia as autonomous entity; NATO officials say Kosovo could wind up as separate entity under international protection if allies succeed in subduing Serbian military (M)

1999-03-31.title: U.N. Panel Seeks to Ease Suffering of Iraq's People
1999-03-31.abstract: Panel of senior UN officials concludes that Iraq has gone from relative affluence to massive poverty since imposition of economic sanctions after Persian Gulf war in 1991 and recommends several steps to alleviate suffering of its people; does not recommend lifting of sanctions, but suggests easing them with specific modifications, which US says it might oppose (M)

1999-03-31.title: Hillary Clinton, in Morocco, Says NATO Attack Aims at Stopping Bloodshed
1999-03-31.abstract: Hillary Rodham Clinton, who is in Marrakesh, Morocco, says that NATO bombing in Yugoslavia is necessary because Serbs planned 'campaign of massive and systematic destruction' against ethnic Albanians in Kosovo; photo (M)
